commitResults.messageTitle: Implement a GUI to the chatbot using JavaFX technology

Refactor parser/storage validation and align parser tests Task parsing and persistence paths contain gaps that can hide defects or surface low-level exceptions. Event input accepts incomplete or invalid date-time ranges too late, storage saving assumes a parent directory always exists, and date parsing does not consistently reject blank input with domain-specific errors. The parser test suite also expects a removed find command API, which breaks test compilation. These issues reduce reliability and make failures harder to interpret. The code should fail with clear domain behavior for user errors and fail fast for impossible internal states. Let's, • validate blank date/date-time input with consistent InvalidInputException messages • enforce event start/end presence and chronological ordering in parsing so invalid ranges are rejected as user input errors • make storage directory creation safe when the path has no parent • reject unsupported task subtypes during serialization instead of silently degrading to a todo format • align parser tests with supported commands by asserting unknown commands are rejected This keeps user-facing validation at parser/date boundaries while preserving assertions for internal invariants. It also improves test signal by matching the actual command surface, allowing the suite to verify behavior rather than fail at compile time.
Add asserts for core invariants

commitResults.messageTitle: Support natural weekday date parsing Accept inputs like Mon/Monday as the next matching weekday for dates and date-times, while keeping existing ISO formats unchanged. Add tests to cover weekday parsing behavior.
